statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, a target number, and a maximum number of elements to include, find out if there exists a subset of the array that sums up to the target using backtracking. A subset can be selected multiple times. If such a subset exists, return True. Otherwise, return False.
statement.inputFormat: The first line will contain the size of the array. The next line will contain the array elements. The last line will contain the target and the maximum number of elements to include.
statement.notes: This problem requires a backtracking approach with memoization.

constraints.description: Maximum number of elements to include

examples.explanation: The subset [1, 3, 4, 7] exists in the array, and another subset [5, 10] also exists.

hints.text: First hint text: Think about the base case when the target is 0. When there is no number to choose, the remaining sum should be 0.
Second hint text: Don't forget to add a condition to handle when there are no numbers left to choose from.

editorial.title: Correcting Backtracking Search
editorial.intuition: The problem requires a backtracking approach with memoization. We can use a recursive function to track the current sum and the current number.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach can solve this problem, but it has high time complexity due to the multiple recursive calls.
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution uses a recursive function with memoization to avoid repeated calculations.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is a recursive function that uses a memoization dictionary to store the results of subproblems.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution is correct because it checks all possible combinations of numbers and returns True if it finds a combination that sums up to the target.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the base case

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public:     bool dfs(int target, int start, int n, int maxNum, vector<int>& arr, vector<vector<bool>>& dp); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean dfs(int target, int start, int n, int maxNum, int[] arr, boolean[][] dp); }

starterCode.javascript: function Solution() {     var dfs = function(target, start, n, maxNum, arr, dp) {     } }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: bool Solution::dfs(int target, int start, int n, int maxNum, vector<int>& arr, vector<vector<bool>>& dp) {     if (target == 0) return true;     if (start >= n || maxNum <= 0) return false;     if (dp[start][maxNum]) return true;     dp[start][maxNum] = true;     return dfs(target - arr[start], start + 1, n, maxNum - 1, arr, dp) || dfs(target, start + 1, n, maxNum, arr, dp); }
buggyStarterCode.java: public boolean Solution::dfs(int target, int start, int n, int maxNum, int[] arr, boolean[][] dp) {     if (target == 0) return true;     if (start >= n || maxNum <= 0) return false;     if (dp[start][maxNum]) return true;     dp[start][maxNum] = true;     return dfs(target - arr[start], start + 1, n, maxNum - 1, arr, dp) || dfs(target, start + 1, n, maxNum, arr, dp); }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def dfs(self, target, start, n, maxNum, arr, dp):    if target == 0: return True    if start >= n or maxNum <= 0: return False    if dp[start][maxNum]: return True    dp[start][maxNum] = True    return self.dfs(target - arr[start], start + 1, n, maxNum - 1, arr, dp) or self.dfs(target, start + 1, n, maxNum, arr, dp)
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function Solution() {     var dfs = function(target, start, n, maxNum, arr, dp) {     if (target == 0) return true;     if (start >= n || maxNum <= 0) return false;     if (dp[start][maxNum]) return true;     dp[start][maxNum] = true;     return dfs(target - arr[start], start + 1, n, maxNum - 1, arr, dp) || dfs(target, start + 1, n, maxNum, arr, dp); } }

functionSignature.cpp: bool dfs(int target, int start, int n, int maxNum, vector<int>& arr, vector<vector<bool>>& dp);
functionSignature.java: public boolean dfs(int target, int start, int n, int maxNum, int[] arr, boolean[][] dp);
functionSignature.python: def dfs(self, target, start, n, maxNum, arr: list[int], dp: list[list[bool]]) -> bool:
functionSignature.javascript: var dfs = function(target, start, n, maxNum, arr);

solutions.cpp: int main() {     int n;     std::cin >> n;     int m;     std::cin >> m;     int target;     std::cin >> target;     int maxNum;     std::cin >> maxNum;     std::vector<int> arr(n);     std::cin >> arr[0];     for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {         std::cin >> arr[i];     }     std::vector<std::vector<bool>> dp(n, std::vector<bool>(m + 1, false));     Solution s;     bool result = s.dfs(target, 0, n, m, arr, dp);     std::cout << result << std::endl;     return 0; }
solutions.java: public class Solution { public boolean dfs(int target, int start, int n, int maxNum, int[] arr, boolean[][] dp) {     if (target == 0) return true;     if (start >= n) return false;     if (dp[start][maxNum]) return true;     dp[start][maxNum] = true;     return dfs(target - arr[start], start + 1, n, maxNum - 1, arr, dp) || dfs(target, start + 1, n, maxNum, arr, dp); } }
solutions.python: class Solution: def dfs(self, target, start, n, maxNum, arr, dp):    if target == 0: return True    if start >= n: return False    if dp[start][maxNum]: return True    dp[start][maxNum] = True    return self.dfs(target - arr[start], start + 1, n, maxNum - 1, arr, dp) or self.dfs(target, start + 1, n, maxNum, arr, dp)
solutions.javascript: function main() {     var arr = readLine().split(' ').map(Number);     var n = arr.shift();     var m = arr.shift();     var target = arr.shift();     var maxNum = arr.shift();     var dp = new Array(n).fill(false).map(() => new Array(m + 1).fill(false));     var s = new Solution();     var result = s.dfs(target, 0, n, m, arr, dp);     print(result); }
